keyPoints: The 'type' command reveals if a command is a shell built-in, an alias, or an external program.
The 'which' command locates the executable file for an external command by searching the directories listed in the PATH environment variable.
The 'root' user (UID 0) has unrestricted system access and is used for administrative tasks, distinguished by the '#' prompt instead of the standard '$'.
commonMistakes: Assuming 'which' will find shell built-ins or aliases (it only finds executables in the PATH).
Using regular user practices and tools when logged in as root, which can lead to accidental system-wide changes or file permission issues.
